ARTISAN.RecipeRequirementHelp: Recipe requirement: the character must have at least this level in the selected profession.
ARTISAN.DropItemOrUuid: Drop an Item here or use a manual UUID
ARTISAN.CreateOrSelectRecipe: Create a new recipe or select one from the list.
ARTISAN.ForagingActorLevelHelp: The level is read from the selected character. The Gathering list no longer stores a manual level.
ARTISAN.MaxDifferentResourcesHelp: Limits how many different resources can be obtained from one gathering attempt.
ARTISAN.DropItemFromCompendiumOrWorld: Drop Items from compendium or world
ARTISAN.ImportedWeightEditable: Weight imported from the Item, editable
ARTISAN.ToolBonusHelp: They add the character proficiency bonus to the roll only if the character owns them and is proficient
ARTISAN.CreateForagingList: Create a list to start adding resources from compendia.
ARTISAN.CreatureTypeHelp: Used to catalog which creature type the parts come from.
ARTISAN.HarvestActorLevelHelp: The level is read from the selected character. The Hunting list does not store a manual level.
ARTISAN.HarvestNoTargetHelp: No target token is required. Select only the character performing the Hunting action.
ARTISAN.MaxDifferentPartsHelp: Limits how many different parts can be obtained from one Hunting action.
ARTISAN.ConsumeRequiredToolLine: Consume 1 use/quantity of the required tool when a part is extracted.
ARTISAN.ConsumeRequiredToolHelp: Only applies to parts that have a required tool set.
ARTISAN.RarityRiskNote: Ruin risk by rarity: Common 0%, Uncommon 10%, Rare 25%, Very Rare 40%, Legendary 60%.
ARTISAN.RequiredToolTitle: If set, this part can only be obtained if the character owns this tool
ARTISAN.RequiredToolUuidTitle: Required tool UUID to extract this part
ARTISAN.CreateHarvestList: Create a list to start adding parts from compendia.
ARTISAN.SavedOnActorFlags: Professions saved on the character via flags.artisan.professions
ARTISAN.XpRecalculatesLevel: When XP is changed, the level is automatically recalculated using the profession thresholds.
ARTISAN.SelectTokenForProfessions: Select a token on the scene to view and edit profession level and XP.
ARTISAN.ActivityEmptyText: The log will fill when you run Crafting, Gathering, Hunting, import, export, or backup.
ARTISAN.PresetIntro: These packages create ready-made structures to complete with Items from your compendia. They do not overwrite existing elements with the same name.
ARTISAN.SettingsSavedWorld: Settings are saved in the current world.
ARTISAN.SettingsIntro: This screen prepares module balancing without editing code by hand.
ARTISAN.Title: Artisan
ARTISAN.Profession.erborista.Label: Herbalist
ARTISAN.Profession.erborista.Description: Gathers herbs, mushrooms, natural reagents, and medicinal plants.
ARTISAN.Profession.cacciatore.Label: Hunter
ARTISAN.Profession.cacciatore.Descriptio: Tracks prey and recovers hides, meat, bones, and animal materials.
ARTISAN.Profession.minatore.Label: Miner
ARTISAN.Profession.minatore.Description: Extracts ores, stones, raw gems, and rocky materials.
ARTISAN.Profession.boscaiolo.Label: Woodcutter
ARTISAN.Profession.boscaiolo.Description: Gathers timber, resins, bark, and forest materials.
ARTISAN.Profession.pescatore.Label: Fisher
ARTISAN.Profession.pescatore.Description: Gathers fish, shells, algae, and fresh or salt water resources.
ARTISAN.Profession.alchimista.Label: Alchemist
ARTISAN.Profession.alchimista.Descriptio: Finds rare reagents, crystals, essences, and unstable materials.
ARTISAN.Profession.cuoco.Label: Cook
ARTISAN.Profession.cuoco.Description: Searches for edible ingredients, spices, fruit, and food resources.
ARTISAN.Profession.conciatore.Label: Tanner
ARTISAN.Profession.conciatore.Descriptio: Recovers hides, raw leather, tendons, and workable organic materials.
ARTISAN.Profession.fabbro.Label: Blacksmith
ARTISAN.Profession.fabbro.Description: Works metals, weapons, armor, nails, tools, and forge components.
ARTISAN.SelectActorRecipeTitle: Select a character token to see whether it can craft this recipe.
ARTISAN.InterfaceLanguageSettingHint: Choose the language used by Artisan. Foundry System uses Foundry's global language.
ARTISAN.LanguageReloadHint: Artisan language updated. Close and reopen Artisan, or reload the world if needed.
ARTISAN.HelpSubtitle: Quick overview of the module and its main sections.
ARTISAN.HelpHeroText: Artisan is a Foundry VTT suite for crafting, gathering, hunting, and actor professions.
ARTISAN.HelpCraftingText: Create recipes with ingredients, tools, and outputs. Recipes can require a profession and a minimum level from the selected actor.
ARTISAN.HelpForagingText: Build biome-based gathering lists with weighted resources, random quantities, and profession multipliers read from the actor.
ARTISAN.HelpHarvestText: Extract parts from creatures or sources using rarity, ruin risk, required tools, and optional tool consumption.
ARTISAN.HelpProfessionsText: Each actor has XP and levels for professions. Levels unlock recipes and improve gathering results.
ARTISAN.HelpStep1: Create or import recipes, Gathering lists, and Hunting lists.
ARTISAN.HelpStep2: Select an actor token before running crafting, gathering, or hunting.
ARTISAN.HelpStep3: Check Actor Professions to review level, XP, and progression.
ARTISAN.HelpStep4: Use Artisan Backup before major edits or before moving data to another world.
ARTISAN.HelpTipText: Preset packages are templates: after installing them, connect your own Items from compendia via drag and drop or UUID.
ARTISAN.HelpDataText: Recipes, lists, and settings are stored in the world; professions are stored on Actors through flags.artisan.professions.
ARTISAN.ModuleSettingsMenuHint: Open the Artisan settings panel. These options were removed from the main window.
ARTISAN.HelpMenuHint: Open the Artisan help guide from the module settings.
ARTISAN.PresetsMenuHint: Open Artisan preset packages from the module settings.
ARTISAN.MainWindowCleanedHint: Packages, Help and Settings are now available from Configure Settings → Module Settings → Artisan.
ARTISAN.SettingsMenuPackagesHint: Open Artisan preset package management from module settings.
ARTISAN.HelpDialogHeroText: Artisan manages crafting, gathering, hunting, and actor professions. The main window is for working; this guide explains the main features.
ARTISAN.HelpDialogStart1: Open a world, enable Artisan, and select the token of the character who will act.
ARTISAN.HelpDialogStart2: Create or import recipes, Gathering lists, and Hunting lists. Link items by dragging them from compendia or world items, or by entering UUIDs.
ARTISAN.HelpDialogStart3: Open Actor Professions to review XP, level, and profession progression for the selected actor.
ARTISAN.HelpDialogStart4: Use the full backup before moving data between worlds or before major edits.
ARTISAN.HelpDialogCraftingText: Crafting creates items from recipes. Each recipe can have ingredients, tools, outputs, DC, time in minutes, and profession requirements.
ARTISAN.HelpDialogCrafting1: The recipe profession level is a requirement: if the actor lacks the required level, the recipe is locked.
ARTISAN.HelpDialogCrafting2: Ingredients are consumed; outputs are created or stacked when they are identical.
ARTISAN.HelpDialogCrafting3: Output quality depends on the roll margin: Normal, Good, Superior, or Excellent.
ARTISAN.HelpDialogForagingText: Gathering gathers environmental resources from biomes such as forest, mountain, swamp, or cave.
ARTISAN.HelpDialogForaging1: Each resource has random weight, minimum quantity, and maximum quantity.
ARTISAN.HelpDialogForaging2: The actor profession level multiplies the gathered quantity.
ARTISAN.HelpDialogForaging3: Proficient tools add the actor proficiency bonus to the roll, not to quantity.
ARTISAN.HelpDialogHarvestText: Hunting extracts parts from creatures or specific sources without requiring a targeted token.
ARTISAN.HelpDialogHarvest1: Each part can have rarity, weight, minimum quantity, and maximum quantity.
ARTISAN.HelpDialogHarvest2: Rare parts may be ruined after a successful Hunting action, based on rarity.
ARTISAN.HelpDialogHarvest3: A part can require a specific tool, which may also be consumed when the option is enabled.
ARTISAN.HelpDialogProfessionsText: Professions are stored directly on the actor through flags.artisan.professions.
ARTISAN.HelpDialogProfessions1: XP automatically increases the profession level.
ARTISAN.HelpDialogProfessions2: Levels unlock Crafting recipes and improve Gathering/Hunting through multipliers.
ARTISAN.HelpDialogProfessions3: The Actor Professions section lets you edit XP and review progression.
ARTISAN.HelpDialogDataText: Recipes, Gathering lists, Hunting lists, activity log, and settings are world data. Professions are stored on Actors. The full backup exports everything into one JSON for recovery or transfer.
ARTISAN.HelpDialogStart5: Check that ingredients, resources, parts, and tools have valid UUIDs before using them in a live session.
ARTISAN.HelpDialogStart6: Before testing in a new world, export recipes, lists, or a full backup from the original world.
ARTISAN.HelpMiniActorText: Crafting, Gathering, and Hunting read professions, XP, proficiencies, and inventory from the selected character on the scene.
ARTISAN.HelpMiniItemsText: Ingredients, outputs, resources, parts, and tools are linked through drag and drop or UUIDs from world Items and compendia.
ARTISAN.HelpMiniToolsText: Tools add a roll bonus only if the character owns them and is proficient; the bonus is the character proficiency bonus.
ARTISAN.HelpMiniBackupText: Use the full backup before updates, large imports, or major table changes.
ARTISAN.HelpCraftingToolsBonus: Required tools add the actor proficiency bonus to the roll if the actor owns them and is proficient.
ARTISAN.HelpCraftingXp: Each recipe can have custom crafting XP. For example Potion +25 XP, Bandages +3 XP. If the value is 0, Artisan uses the automatic calculation.
ARTISAN.HelpForagingListsText: A Gathering list represents a gathering environment, with biome, DC, time in minutes, and available resources.
ARTISAN.HelpForagingMixedResources: A gathering attempt can obtain multiple different resources randomly, using each resource weight, minimum quantity, and maximum quantity.
ARTISAN.HelpForagingMaxResources: Maximum different resources limits how many resources can appear in one gathering attempt.
ARTISAN.HelpForagingRollText: The selected character rolls the list skill. Profession level changes the gathered quantity.
ARTISAN.HelpForagingCrits: A natural 20 doubles final quantities; a natural 1 fails and can damage or destroy a valid tool.
ARTISAN.HelpHarvestPartsText: Hunting creates tables of extractable parts from creatures or specific sources.
ARTISAN.HelpHarvestCreatureType: Creature type catalogs the list and clarifies which source the parts come from.
ARTISAN.HelpHarvestToolsText: Tools can be optional, required for the list, or required by a single part.
ARTISAN.HelpHarvestConsumableTools: If Consume required tool is enabled, the tool is reduced by 1 or removed when the part is collected.
ARTISAN.HelpHarvestNoTarget: Hunting does not require a target token: select the acting character and choose the list.
ARTISAN.HelpProfessionLevelSource: Levels are not set on lists: they are read from Actors through flags.artisan.professions.
ARTISAN.HelpBackup1: The full backup exports recipes, Gathering, Hunting, and actor professions into one JSON file.
ARTISAN.HelpBackup2: Section import/export is useful when you want to move only recipes or only specific tables.
ARTISAN.HelpBackup3: The activity log helps the GM review recent Crafting, gathering, Hunting, and import/export actions.
ARTISAN.HelpTroubleshooting1: If the Artisan button does not appear, reload the world with CTRL+F5 or restart Foundry.
ARTISAN.HelpTroubleshooting2: If an element is not added, check that the UUID belongs to an accessible Item or drag it again from the compendium.
ARTISAN.HelpTroubleshooting3: If a tool gives no bonus, check that the actor owns it and is proficient with that tool.
ARTISAN.HelpTroubleshooting4: If a recipe is locked, check required profession, required level, and saved actor professions.
ARTISAN.SelectActorForProfessionRead: Select a token to read the profession from the character
ARTISAN.ToolProficiencyRollBonusTitle: Roll bonus applied only if the character owns the tool and is proficient with it
ARTISAN.PresetForagingBaseSubtitle: Biomes ready to fill with resources from your compendia.
ARTISAN.PresetForagingBaseDescription: Creates Forest, Mountain, Swamp, Coast, Cave, and Desert lists with DC, time, profession, and maximum resources already configured.
ARTISAN.PresetHarvestBaseSubtitle: Hunting tables ready for common creature types.
ARTISAN.PresetHarvestBaseDescription: Creates lists for Beasts, Dragons, Undead, Monstrosities, and Plants. Parts still need to be linked to your Items/compendia.
ARTISAN.PresetRecipeTemplatesSubtitle: Empty recipes already divided by profession.
ARTISAN.PresetRecipeTemplatesDescription: Creates base models for Alchemist, Blacksmith, Cook, Herbalist, Tanner, and Woodcutter, ready for ingredients and outputs.
ARTISAN.SettingProfessionXpDescription: Allows Artisan to award profession XP during Crafting, Gathering, and Hunting.
ARTISAN.SettingOutputQualityDescription: Allows created item quality to be applied based on the roll margin.
ARTISAN.SettingToolDamageDescription: Allows Gathering and Hunting to damage or destroy tools on a natural 1.
ARTISAN.SettingHarvestRuinRiskDescriptio: Allows rare Hunting parts to be ruined based on rarity.
ARTISAN.SettingActivityLogDescription: Records Artisan activity in the Activity Log section.
ARTISAN.Subtitle: Universal Crafting System
ARTISAN.ProfessionAlreadyMaxLevel: This profession is already at maximum level.
ARTISAN.HarvestOutputModeHelp: Choose whether to obtain a random selection of parts or every configured available part. In all-parts mode, weight/probability does not filter parts: a cow can yield meat + hide in the same Hunting action.
ARTISAN.CraftingXpHelp: Profession XP gained for each crafted lot. Use 0 for the automatic calculation based on required level. A natural 20 doubles XP.
ARTISAN.QualityFormulaPathHelp: Leave empty to auto-detect healing, damage, or system.formula. If a path is set, the improvement is applied there.
ARTISAN.QualityBonusHelp: For each quality tier you can set a flat bonus, an extra die, and an effect type. Examples: Good +1 healing, Superior +1d4 fire, Excellent +1d6 poison. Normal does not modify the item.
ARTISAN.QualityDiceHelp: Example: 1d4, 1d6, 2d4. Leave empty for no die.
ARTISAN.SettingToolDamageCraftingDescrip: Allows Crafting to damage or destroy tools on a natural 1.
ARTISAN.SettingToolDamageForagingDescrip: Allows Gathering to damage or destroy tools on a natural 1.
ARTISAN.SettingToolDamageHarvestDescript: Allows Hunting to damage or destroy tools on a natural 1.
ARTISAN.SettingToolDamageDisassemblyDesc: Allows Disassembly to damage or destroy tools on a natural 1.
ARTISAN.ToolDamageDisabled: Tool damage is disabled for this section.
